message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Mr. Lyons (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~797 reviews; Tripadvisor ~281 reviews; OpenTable ~1,888 reviews (combined ~3,000)
- Sentiment: mostly positive but polarized — strong on atmosphere/cocktails/service; recurring value complaints
- Longevity: Building lineage from Lyons English Grille (1945); reopened as Mr. Lyons May 2015 under Foundation 10 Creative (Tara Lazar); ~11 years under current ownership
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life 'Best Steakhouse' editor pick 2021","Palm Springs Life 'Best Steakhouse' editor pick 2022","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","Gayot rating 14/20","The Infatuation 8.0/10","Featured in Palm Springs Life 'Restaurants With Great Steak' guide"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best steakhouse Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs', 'fine dining Palm Springs', 'romantic restaurants Palm Springs'. Less dominant against chains (Mastro's Palm Desert, Eddie V's, The Edge at Ritz-Carlton, Steakhouse at Agua Caliente) in broader 'Coachella Valley steakhouse' queries. The Infatuation positions Bar Cecil as preferred date-night pick, signaling some erosion at the top.
- Site structured data: Own site (mrlyonsf10.com) is powered by Popmenu (evident from /popmenu-order/ URL pattern), which typically emits Restaurant schema.org JSON-LD, structured menus, and hours. Multiple dedicated menu pages (dining-main-web, happy-hour-web, event-menu) — clean for agents. WebFetch returned 403 (anti-bot), suggesting aggressive blocking that may hurt agent crawlability despite good underlying structure.
- Visibility gap score: 4
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"THE 10 BEST Steakhouses in Palm Springs (Updated 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32847-c37-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"2026 Best Steakhouses Palm Springs | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/best-palmsprings-ca-top10-steakhouses_35ps.html"},{"title":"THE BEST 10 STEAKHOUSES in PALM SPRINGS, CA - Updated 2026 - Hours - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?cflt=steak&find_loc=Palm+Springs,+CA"},{"title":"32 Best Steakhouses In Palm Springs, Palm Springs / Palm Desert Cities | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/cuisine/best-steakhouse-restaurants-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"Steakhouses | Vacation Palm Springs","url":"https://vacationpalmsprings.com/restaurants/steakhouses"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Steakhouse in Palm Springs, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Steakhouse&find_loc=Palm+Springs,+CA"},{"title":"11 Best Steakhouses in Palm Springs, CA for 2026 (Top Places!)","url":"https://familydestinationsguide.com/best-steakhouses-in-palm-springs-ca/"},{"title":"THE 5 BEST Steakhouses in Palm Desert (Updated 2026) - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32846-c37-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Best Steakhouses in Palm Springs You Need to Try","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/palm-springs-steakhouses/"},{"title":"Top 5 steakhouses in Palm Springs, april 2026 - Restaurant Guru","url":"https://restaurantguru.com/steakhouse-Palm-Springs-c179"}]

Based on the 2026 search results, here are some of the best steakhouses in Palm Springs:

**Top-Rated Steakhouses:**

Mr. Lyons is a beacon of exceptional American cuisine with accolades like "Editors pick for best steakhouse" from Palm Springs Life in 2021 and 2022. Reviewers rave about the "juicy, flavorful" steaks and the "life-changing" ribeye with bone marrow butter.

Ruth's Chris Steak House has been a Palm Desert favorite among both locals and visitors winning several awards, including 'Best Steak House in the Desert' for nine consecutive years. Ruth's Chris specializes in serving USDA prime steaks and they are known for their steaks topped with butter and served to sizzle on a 500-degree plate.

The Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage is renowned for its exquisite steaks and seafood and is located within The Ritz-Carlton, offering a dining experience with breathtaking views.

**Other Notable Options:**

G's Prime Steakhouse is a premier destination for USDA Prime steaks, boasting an on-site dry-aging facility and an extensive wine list with over 500 selections.

Morton's offers steak, seafood and a great happy hour menu, and Churrasco Brazilian Steakhouse offers a traditional Brazilian steakhouse experience in Palm Springs.

LYONS STEAKHOUSE in Palm Springs, CA | Indeed.com","url":"https://www.indeed.com/cmp/Mr.-Lyons-Steakhouse/reviews?fcountry=US&floc=Palm+Springs,+CA&fjobcat=food"}]

Based on the search results, here's what reviews reveal about Mr. Lyons Palm Springs steakhouse:

## Overview
Mr. Lyons was established in 2015, though it has been around in some form since the 1940s and underwent a massive renovation in 2015, retaking its place as the desert's go-to spot for breaking out your best pearls, sipping martinis, and enjoying giant slabs of meat.

## Atmosphere & Service
The restaurant features an elegant dining salon with personalized tableside service, classic cocktails in the lounge, and private dining rooms and patio seating. It feels completely old-school but with modern updates, with horseshoe-shaped banquettes covered in seafoam velvet and double-olive garnishes ferried to white linen-covered tables.

## Food Quality
Reviews are mixed. Some diners praise the old fashioned steakhouse atmosphere and note the steaks are among the best they've had, with excellent cocktails and service. However, other reviews note that the quality of steaks was rather poor and they weren't cooked properly.

## Overall Rating
On Tripadvisor, Mr. Lyons is rated 3.9 of 5 and ranked #99 of 308 restaurants in Palm Springs.

Links: [{"title":"Interview: Tara Lazar (Foundation 10 Creative, Palm Springs)","url":"https://foodgps.com/interview-chef-tara-lazar-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons | Palm Springs Venue | PartySlate","url":"https://www.partyslate.com/venues/mr-lyons"},{"title":"Jesse Bluma at Pointe Viven: Where to Toast and Dine: Mister Lyons and Seymour’s Review by Jesse Bluma at Pointe Viven","url":"http://pointeviven.blogspot.com/2017/07/Where-to-Toast-and-Dine-Mister-Lyons-Mr-David-Lyons-Seymours-Review-Jesse-Bluma-Pointe-Viven-Zomato-Number-1-One-Blog-Tara-Lazar-Foundation-10.html"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse Reimagines Iconic 1940s Lyons English Grille in Palm Springs | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/mr-lyons-steakhouse-reimagines-iconic-1940s-lyons-english-grille-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"F10 Creative - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/tag/f10-creative/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/tag/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"Mister Lyons Palm Springs - Palm Springs, United States","url":"https://yellow.place/en/mister-lyons-palm-springs-palm-springs-usa"},{"title":"F10 Creative | LinkedIn","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/company/f10-creative"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Mr. Lyons and its connection to Tara Lazar and Foundation 10 Creative in 2015:

Mr. Lyons was reimagined in 2015 by Tara Lazar as part of Foundation 10 Creative. The space was previously family-owned and operated for over 70 years by David Lyons as Lyons English Grill. 

Foundation 10 Creative took over the South Palm Springs space from the original owner, David Lyons, and reopened as Mr. Lyons Steakhouse in May 2015. Foundation 10 Creative is a collective owned by restaurateur and hotelier Tara Lazar, the local force behind well-known Palm Springs restaurants, Cheeky's and Birba, as well as Alcazar hotel.

Links: [{"title":"A little history - Mr. Lyons Steakhouse | Steak House | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.mrlyonsf10.com/about"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/mr-lyons-palm-springs"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons : Palm Springs | Xtreme Foodies - The world's Essential Eats curated by local food experts","url":"https://www.xtremefoodies.com/north-america/palm-springs/review/Mr-Lyons/Mr-Lyons/9060_9336"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse – COACHELLA MAGAZINE","url":"https://coachellamagazine.com/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse | Palm Springs, California, United States | Venue Report","url":"https://www.venuereport.com/venue/mr-lyons-steakhouse/"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons - Steakhouse Restaurant in Palm Springs, CA | The Vendry","url":"https://thevendry.com/venue/110825/mr.-lyons-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"Mr. Lyons Steakhouse - Steak house in Palm Springs ...","url":"https://www.postcard.inc/places/mr-lyons-palm-springs"},{"title":"Palm Springs restaurants | Mr. Lyons Steakhouse Reimagines Iconic 1940s Lyons English Grille in Palm Springs","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/mr-lyons-steakhouse-reimagines-iconic-1940s-lyons-english-grille-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Palm Springs Weekend - Le Continental","url":"https://deanjab.com/2013/12/palm-springs-weekend/"}]

Based on the search results, here's the history of Mr. Lyons and Lyons English Grille:

Lyons English Grille was originally opened in 1945, and it was originally family-owned and operated by David Lyons for over 70 years. This Olde English restaurant was opened in 1945 by the Lyons family, with David Lyons as the owner since the opening.

The iconic restaurant was formerly known for its 19th century English décor dating back to the 1940s. The decor featured coats of arms, heraldic flags, suits of armor, large stained glass portraits and scenes, and much bric-à-brac, much of which was collected by David Lyons, who is originally from England.

The restaurant underwent significant changes in recent years: In 2014, the building was purchased from David Lyons, and after a complete renovation, it was reborn in 2015 as Mr. Lyons, a tribute to its original owner. Mr. Lyons seamlessly combines the timeless allure of a classic steakhouse with a modern twist, evoking a bygone era where jazz standards, martinis, and premium steaks ruled the scene.

Based on the search results, Mr. Lyons Steakhouse is a steakhouse restaurant in Palm Springs that GAYOT.com awards a rating of 14 on a 20-point scale.

The restaurant is described as classic and contemporary, with an old-school aesthetic but modern updates. It features horseshoe-shaped banquettes covered in seafoam velvet and tulip-shaped glasses and double-olive garnishes served to white linen-covered tables.

According to the GAYOT review, starters include roasted beef marrow bones and steak tartare, the lobster Louis salad is chopped with chunks of Maine lobster but drizzled with flavorless dressing, and the 12-ounce rib-eye was nicely grilled. The wine list is extensive, with mostly California varietals.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent date night restaurant options in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Romantic Fine Dining

Copley's is an award-winning restaurant serving American cuisine infused with hints of tropical and European flavors, located in part of the former Cary Grant estate in the heart of Palm Springs. It's open 7 days a week through June 2026.

Melvyn's at The Ingleside Inn is known for their white cloth dining and is one of the last restaurants in Greater Palm Springs to offer tableside cooking with bygone favorites like Steak Diane.

Mélange at The Chateau Lake La Quinta is the only spot to enjoy classic fare with a lake view, and you can request a table on the patio to enjoy gorgeous views of Lake La Quinta while dining.

## Contemporary & Rooftop Dining

4 Saints located on the rooftop of The Kimpton Rowan in Palm Springs offers an innovative approach to fine dining with a selection of craft cocktails and delightful wines.

Il Corso in Palm Springs offers an authentic Italian dining experience, with reviews praising its 'beautiful ambiance' and 'excellent food and service', making it perfect for a date night.

## Casual to Upscale

FARM is a traditional Provencial-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with a menu comprised of traditional European dishes, brimmed with locally sourced produce and artisanal meats, where the prix fixe dinner menu is served at a pace so you may enjoy your evening surrounded by a beautiful outdoor setting.

For special occasion dining, Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week offers restaurants across the city with special prix fixe menus and exclusive seasonal dishes.

## The Infatuation Review

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Bar Cecil and The Infatuation's date night review:

Bar Cecil is The Infatuation's favorite place for date night in Palm Springs. 

Reservations at this American bar/restaurant get nabbed weeks in advance—even during the low season. However, the bar area is walk-in only and that's where you'll want to sit anyway. The entire menu is offered at the bar, and The Infatuation recommends getting the pork chop.

Bar Cecil is one of those places that's sexy and chic without feeling like "sexy and chic" is the theme. It's also The Infatuation's favorite place to grab dinner in town.

The Infatuation review was published on May 10, 2024, and can be found on their website at theinfatuation.com.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Mr. Lyons in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Mr. Lyons is a casual elegant steakhouse that features steakhouse classics with a modern edge. Originally opened as Lyons English Grille in 1945, it was purchased and renovated, reopening in 2015 as Mr. Lyons.

## Romantic Atmosphere
It's a classic, old-school steakhouse with a glamorous, retro vibe featuring velvet booths, jazz standards, dim lighting, and well-dressed guests, with many guests calling it romantic and sexy. This women-owned establishment provides a romantic atmosphere and impeccable service.

## Fine Dining Experience
It's a classic steakhouse with a menu that puts local small farms first, delivered with impeccable tableside service. It was an Editors pick for best steakhouse in "Palm Springs Life 2021 and 2022."

## Menu & Features
The diverse menu boasts vegan and limited vegetarian options. The wine list is extensive with mostly California varietals, and there's a lounge with live entertainment and a rousing bar scene on weekends.

## Location & Hours
The address is 233 E Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92264. It's open Wednesday-Sunday from 6 PM - 10 PM with happy hour from 6 PM - 7 PM.

content.text: The 403 confirms the aggressive blocking. I have enough verification. Let me finalize.

confirms: Yelp ~797 reviews (confirmed directly in Yelp page title: '631 Photos & 797 Reviews')
Tripadvisor ~281 reviews (confirmed via Tripadvisor fetch; rating 3.9/5)
OpenTable ~1,888 reviews at 4.4 stars (confirmed via search snippet 'rated 4.4 stars by 1888 OpenTable diners')
Building lineage from Lyons English Grille 1945, run by David Lyons family for 70+ years
Reopened May 2015 as Mr. Lyons under Tara Lazar's Foundation 10 Creative
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award (confirmed via Tripadvisor page fetch — badge displayed)
Gayot rating of 14/20 (confirmed in Gayot.com listing)
The Infatuation 8.0/10 rating (confirmed by direct WebFetch of theinfatuation.com review page)
Palm Springs Life 'Editors pick for best steakhouse' 2021 and 2022
Surfaces reliably for 'best steakhouse Palm Springs' (appears in Gayot, Tripadvisor, Yelp, family-destinations top-lists)
Bar Cecil is The Infatuation's preferred date-night/dinner pick in Palm Springs — confirms 'erosion at the top' framing
Own site mrlyonsf10.com returns HTTP 403 to WebFetch — confirms aggressive anti-bot blocking that hurts agent crawlability
Sentiment is polarized — atmosphere/cocktails/service praised; recurring quality/value complaints visible in Yelp/Tripadvisor/Gayot ('flavorless dressing', 'steaks poor/not cooked properly')
contradicts: Claim states '~11 years under current ownership' — actually closer to 11 years counting from May 2015 to June 2026, so this is technically accurate (11.1 years). No contradiction, but if rounded as of report_date it should read '11 years' exactly.

Date-night dominance claim is softer than stated: in broader 'date night Palm Springs 2026' results, Mr. Lyons does NOT surface in top editorial picks — Copley's, Melvyn's, 4 Saints, Il Corso, FARM lead. Mr. Lyons appears in steakhouse-specific and romantic-restaurant lists but is not a top organic date-night result. Claim that it 'surfaces reliably for date night Palm Springs' is overstated.
flagged_as_unverified: Featured in Palm Springs Life 'Restaurants With Great Steak' guide — could not locate this specific guide title in searches; Palm Springs Life coverage exists but the named guide was not directly verified

Popmenu integration on mrlyonsf10.com — could not verify the /popmenu-order/ URL pattern or JSON-LD emission directly because the site returned 403 to all fetch attempts; the structural assessment is inferred, not independently confirmed
Specific menu-page URLs (dining-main-web, happy-hour-web, event-menu) — not independently verified due to 403 blocking

Combined ~3,000 reviews — math holds (797+281+1888 = 2,966) but assumes no double-counting and excludes Google/Facebook (Facebook shows 1,167 reviews per search snippet, suggesting actual cross-platform footprint is larger)
input.notes: Core review counts, credentials, and longevity all independently confirmed. The 403 from mrlyonsf10.com is real and corroborates the crawlability concern — but it also means the Popmenu/JSON-LD claim is unverified rather than confirmed. Visibility gap score of 4 seems reasonable: the entity is well-indexed across third-party review platforms (which agents heavily consume) but its own canonical site is gated, and Bar Cecil clearly outranks it in editorial date-night coverage. The date-night surfacing claim is the weakest part of the assessment — Mr. Lyons does well in steakhouse/romantic queries but is not a top organic date-night pick in current editorial sources.
